title: " no handler for negotiate authentication"
body:
Attempting to interact with a private V2 registry, with creds provided in either a config.json or via command line, I receive:

```
sudo skopeo inspect --creds=username:password docker://private-registry/imagename:tag 
FATA[0000] no handler for negotiate authentication  
```

I'd run into the same error with Docker 1.12 using Fedora 25, and found this:  https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/30276#issuecomment-279368196

That worked around the issue for Docker itself.  Is this the same issue, and is there a workaround for Skopeo?  The thread suggests it's something in the RHEL version of the Docker rpm, so maybe this is something else.

Just taking a guess, I tried `skopeo --insecure-policy ... `,  as well as setting up a policy of type `insecureAcceptAnything` for the docker transport, but both resulted in the same error.  I suspect I may be barking up the wrong tree.

comments.body:
@clcollins which skopeo and docker version are you using? please create an issue over at projectatomic/docker if there's an issue with docker though.
For skopeo, can you try a newer release? or even master?

@runcom Thanks for the quick reply.  I was using the skopeo RPM from Fedora:

```
/usr/bin/skopeo -v
skopeo version 0.1.22 commit: c7d5a8c5cd84581154281e12ecb683fd69282041
```
I built from source and tried with that, with the same result:

```
[chris@krang skopeo] $ /usr/bin/skopeo -v
skopeo version 0.1.23-dev commit: 91e801b45115580c0709905719ae14c42f201027
[chris@krang skopeo] $   sudo skopeo inspect  docker://private-repo/my-image:tag
FATA[0000] no handler for negotiate authentication 
```

Our V2 registry is using Kerberos auth, and I do have a config.json in ~/.docker/config.json with the creds stored.

In the webserver logs for the registry, I can see the connection attempt receives a 401 response when using `kopeo inspect` but a `docker pull` works fine using those same creds.

I don't think there's an issue with Docker, unless it's with the V2 registry itself.  With the workaround you provided in the other thread, Docker has been working fine.  I'm not even 100% sure these are related, other than the same wording in the error.   

My local docker version:
```
[chris@krang ~] $ docker -v
Docker version 1.12.6, build ae7d637/1.12.6
```

I'm happy to provide more info if you need it.

Yeah, the containers/image client for docker/distribution servers currently doesnât support Kerberos at all, in any version of the client.

The `--signature-verification=false` option to `docker` works around this by disabling the code which uses containers/image, but no amount of options to containers/image can avoid using that client (other than not using a docker/distribution at all, or changing the authentication scheme).

If you really need Kerberos support, then Iâm afraid thereâs no workaround currently; the code for that support has to be written.

---

But, are you really _using_ the Kerberos support? From a quick look I can't see that Docker supports `negotiate` either. The `--creds=username:password` argument also suggests that the Kerberos support isnât actually needed.

In that case it seems to just be fairly trivial problem in `dockerClient.setupRequestAuth`: it always uses the first challenge, whether supported or not, and chokes on an unsupported one; it should instead skip the unsupported ones as long as at least one challenge is supported.

@mtrmac Yes, I'm sorry, I was clouded the issue by mentioning Kerberos.  We have an Apache server in front of the registry, and are using mod_auth_kerb, which I understand uses basic auth between the client and the apache server.  So from Skopeo's point of view, it should look like basic auth, I think.

My apologies for introducing unnecessary info.

No, it was actually good to mention Kerberos as a legitimate part of the picture.

To maximize the likelihood that a fix works for you, can you paste the full output of `curl -i https://private-registry/v2/`, please? Feel free to replace the host names and other private information, but keep the structure and syntax intact.

Sure, here's the output of the curl command (w/o creds): ``` $ curl -i https://private-registry/v2/ HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized Date: Mon, 17 Jul 2017 18:30:18 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.6 (CentOS) OpenSSL/1.0.1e-fips mod_auth_kerb/5.4 Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0 WWW-Authenticate: Negotiate WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="Kerberos Login" Content-Length: 381 Content-Type: text/html; charset=iso-8859-1 401 Unauthorized

Unauthorized

This server could not verify that you are authorized to access the document requested. Either you supplied the wrong credentials (e.g., bad password), or your browser doesn't understand how to supply the credentials required.

```
Working beautifully with our use case.  Thanks for the quick turnaround!
@runcom should we cut a new version?
Very likely, I'll try to cut a new skopeo possibly after dinner
will wait for image-spec v1 to land in c/image before releasing :tada:
